message:
fix(raptorq): remove explicit OG/Twitter metadata to enable file-convention image tags

Next.js was not generating cache-busted image URLs (e.g.
opengraph-image?hash) because explicit openGraph and twitter objects
in the metadata export were overriding the file convention. The
/about page works because it has NO explicit OG/twitter metadata,
letting Next.js auto-detect opengraph-image.tsx and twitter-image.tsx
and generate proper meta tags with type, width, height, and cache
query params.

Removed the openGraph and twitter metadata objects entirely. The
page title and description fields remain, and Next.js will merge
them with the auto-generated image tags from the file convention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
